methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Carbyon is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised €22.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage companies in Climate & Energy, supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private comparables and 1 public reference (LNZA). The immediate peer set comprises Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a $150M implied valuation. This reflects the median across the three stage-matched private comparables, with a range spanning $110M (25th percentile) to $200M (75th percentile). No revenue multiples, DCF, or cost-of-capital analyses were available to cross-validate this estimate via alternative methods.

The triangulated valuation range is $128M–$172M, anchored to a median of $150M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodological approach. Key uncertainties include technology maturity and path to commercialization, which are not quantified in the available data. The public market reference and expanded private comparables (including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials) provide market context but were not directly incorporated into the point estimate due to stage or sector mismatch. Investors should require clarity on unit economics and near-term revenue visibility before anchoring final pricing.
